channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US officials are bound for Europe for top-level talks on Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump’s national security adviser said Sunday that top administration officials will meet with European officials this week about how to end the war in Ukraine, nearly three years after Russia launched an all-out invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ecuador votes for president, with the conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer in the lead
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador’s presidential election Sunday is shaping up to be a repeat of the 2023 race, when voters chose a young, conservative millionaire over the leftist protégée of the country’s most influential president this century.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Noem confident in legality of housing migrants at Guantánamo Bay
item.description: Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said she’s confident that steps the government is taking to deport certain migrants from the U.S. mainland to Guantánamo Bay are legal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: UN humanitarian chief says Gaza ceasefire has averted famine but any truce collapse brings danger
item.description: The United Nations humanitarian chief says famine has been mostly averted in Gaza as a surge of aid enters the territory during a fragile ceasefire, but he warns the threat could return quickly if the truce collapses.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rescuers recover 1 body and search for 28 people in a landslide in southwest China
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Emergency teams in China's southwestern Sichuan province raced against time Sunday to locate 28 people after a landslide triggered by rains killed one person and buried homes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: They helped US order airstrikes against Taliban. Now Trump's moves have left those Afghans in limbo
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — They helped the U.S. military order airstrikes against Taliban and Islamic State fighters and worked as drivers and translators during America's longest war. They were set to start new lives in the United States.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sam Nujoma, Namibia's fiery freedom fighter and first president, dies aged 95
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Sam Nujoma, the fiery, white-bearded freedom fighter who led Namibia to independence from apartheid South Africa in 1990 and served as its first president for 15 years, coming to be known as the father of his nation, has died. He was 95.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, FARAI MUTSAKA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dalai Lama's elder brother, who led several rounds of talks with China, dies at 97
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The elder brother of the Dalai Lama and former chairman of the Tibetan government-in-exile in India, Gyalo Thondup, who led several rounds of talks with China and worked with foreign governments for the Tibetan cause, has died. He was 97.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kosovo votes for new parliament as foreign aid dwindles and talks with Serbia are stalled
item.description: Kosovars are casting votes in a parliamentary election considered a key test for Prime Minister Albin Kurti as talks on normalizing ties with rival Serbia remain stalled and foreign funding in one of Europe’s poorest countries in question.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Released Thai hostages return to Bangkok after being held for over a year in Gaza
item.description: Five Thai workers released after being held hostage for over a year in Gaza arrived in Bangkok on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Leaders from eastern and southern Africa call for a ceasefire and negotiations in Congo
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Leaders from eastern and southern Africa on Saturday called for an immediate ceasefire in eastern Congo, where rebels are threatening to overthrow the Congolese government, but also urged Congo's president to directly negotiate with them.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump must not repeat his Kim Jong Un mistake with Iran, security expert warns
item.description: President Trump revealed this week he wants to pursue talks with Iran to end its nuclear program, but one expert says it could be a repeat of when Trump looked to take on North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Hamas names next 3 Israeli hostages to be exchanged for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: Hamas named the next three Israeli hostages it plans to release this weekend as part of the Gaza ceasefire deal with Israel. It was a sign that the first phase of the truce was moving forward as planned -- even as many observers fear that U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Autocratic regimes 'relishing' USAID gutting: Samantha Power
item.description: Samantha Power, a former administrator of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), says autocratic regimes across the world are “relishing” the Trump administration's efforts to gut the agency. “It's not even an opinion.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Frozen US aid brings new challenges to groups hit by harsh crackdowns in Russia and Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Rights groups, activists and independent media in Russia and Belarus endured increased government scrutiny, repressive laws and even being outlawed and forced to operate from exile abroad. Many survived despite the difficulties.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Egypt lobbies against Trump plan to empty Gaza of Palestinians as Israel prepares for it
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel says it has begun preparations for the departure of Palestinians from Gaza despite international rejection of President Donald Trump’s plan to empty the territory of its population.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy to lead Ukraine’s delegation at Munich Security Conference which JD Vance will attend
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will lead Ukraine’s delegation at the Munich Security Conference next week, which U.S. Vice President J.D.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu gifts Trump controversial item that helped turn tide in war against Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu gave President Trump an unusual gift in homage to the controversial pager operation that targeted Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy wants nukes or NATO; Trump special envoy Kellogg says 'slim and none' chance
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv should get NATO membership or nukes to defend against Russia as Trump's special envoy heads to Europe for talks with allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arab nations decry Trump proposal for US to take over Gaza
item.description: Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for the United Nations to “protect the Palestinian people.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Earthquakes are rattling Greece's volcanic island of Santorini: What we know
item.description: Although Greece is no stranger to earthquakes, seismic activity of such frequency and intensity without having been preceded by a major quake is unusual, seismologists say.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's suggestion the US 'take over' the Gaza Strip is rejected by allies and adversaries alike
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's proposal that the United States “take over” the Gaza Strip and permanently resettle its Palestinian residents was swiftly rejected and denounced on Wednesday by American allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: USPS resumes accepting packages from China, Hong Kong
item.description: USPS says it's working with CBP to implement a collection process for new China tariffs to avoid delivery disruptions.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Saudis could 'de-radicalize' Palestinians: Trump envoy
item.description: “We’ll have a lot of clarity for you in the coming days,” a Trump envoy says of questions about Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation
